evals.name: fast-moving-factual-recency
evals.prompt: what are the current claude model ids and which one should i default to for an agent that mostly does tool calling? i keep seeing different names in blog posts and half of them seem outdated
evals.expected_output: Current model IDs verified against primary Anthropic sources (not blog posts), claims dated, a concrete default recommendation with reasoning, outdated names from blogs explicitly flagged as stale.

assertions.text: Cites a primary Anthropic source (docs.anthropic.com / platform.claude.com / anthropic.com), not only blogs or aggregators
States when each claim was verified or the publication date of sources (recency discipline)
Gives one concrete default recommendation rather than an undifferentiated list
Output includes a Sources section with working references

evals.name: codebase-exploration-ground-truth
evals.prompt: how does auth actually work in ~/Projects/arboreus-api? like where do roles get enforced - is it the API layer or the database? I need to know before I add a new reviewer-only endpoint
evals.expected_output: Correctly identifies Supabase Auth + JWT, the role set (learner/creator/reviewer/admin), and RLS policies as the DB-layer enforcement, with real file paths as evidence. Answers the layering question directly and gives actionable guidance for the new endpoint.

assertions.text: Identifies Supabase Auth with JWT and RLS policy enforcement (verifiable against the repo)
Every architectural claim cites a file path that actually exists in the repo
Directly answers the API-layer vs database-layer question rather than describing both vaguely
No fabricated file paths or invented middleware names

evals.name: consumer-seo-resistance
evals.prompt: my wrists hurt after long coding sessions. looking for a split ergonomic keyboard, ideally under $250, that real people have used for a year plus and still recommend. not interested in whatever amazon is pushing
evals.expected_output: Recommendations sourced from specialist communities (r/ErgoMechKeyboards, geekhack, long-term review blogs) with longevity signal, not SEO listicles. Picks triangulated across 2+ independent sources, contradictions flagged, confidence levels stated.

assertions.text: At least one source is a specialist community or long-term user report (forum, subreddit, enthusiast blog), not a commerce/SEO listicle
Each recommended product is supported by 2+ independent sources
Findings carry confidence levels and a Sources section
Addresses the longevity criterion (year-plus ownership signal) rather than launch reviews

evals.name: contested-question-contradiction-handling
evals.prompt: is it actually safe to run postgres in kubernetes for production in 2026 or is that still a bad idea? getting conflicting advice from my team. we're a 3 person startup, ~50GB database
evals.expected_output: Presents the genuine disagreement (operator maturity vs operational complexity) with sources on both sides, identifies the context that makes each side right, and lands on a sized recommendation for a 3-person/50GB context instead of flattening to one camp.

assertions.text: Represents both positions with at least one source each (does not flatten the controversy)
Explains the context-dependence (team size, ops maturity, data size) that resolves the contradiction
Gives a concrete recommendation for the stated context (3-person startup, 50GB)
Sources are dated or recency-checked (operator landscape changed substantially 2023-2026)

evals.name: negative-evidence-and-stopping
evals.prompt: I want a CLI tool that automates UI testing for Compose Multiplatform desktop apps - something like playwright but for compose. what are my options?
evals.expected_output: Correctly concludes the market is near-empty (no direct Playwright-equivalent for Compose Multiplatform), treats the absence as a finding with evidence of where it looked, surfaces the nearest alternatives and their gaps, rather than padding weak matches into a fake comparison.

assertions.text: States clearly that no direct equivalent exists (negative finding presented as the answer, not hedged away)
Lists where it looked (search coverage as evidence for the negative claim)
Nearest-alternative tools are described with their actual gaps, not inflated into equivalents
Does not fabricate a tool or misrepresent an Android-only/Espresso-based tool as Compose Multiplatform desktop support
